methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Sabanto is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private and 1 public comparable, including same-sector stage-adjacent peers such as 1X Technologies (Series B, $10.0B), Bedrock Robotics (Series B, $1.8B), and Dexterity (Series B, $1.6B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $20M total raised as a base proxy, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, yielding $40M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B and a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in robotics subsectors and capital efficiency.

The triangulated valuation range is $34M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $558M. The material gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects typical Series A pricing friction: Sabanto's early-stage capital raised sits well below the typical valuation multiple observed in the peer set at more advanced stages. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue traction, capital efficiency relative to peer benchmarks, and whether peer valuations sustain in a tightening funding environment. The median estimate of $558M assumes Sabanto's execution risk and market position warrant a valuation closer to earlier-stage peers within the robotics cohort.
